title: veronica comosa var. glandulosa
intro: Veronica comosa var. glandulosa, also known as the glandular broomrape, is a plant species in the borage family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Veronica comosa var. glandulosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
